Week 1 Exercise

LI XINYUE

2026-04-16

  1. Distribution of Mathematics Scores
ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 15, fill = "lightblue", color = "black")

Insight: The histogram shows the distribution of Mathematics scores. Most students are concentrated in the middle range.

  1. Distribution by Gender
ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, colour = GENDER)) +
  geom_density()

Insight: The distribution between male and female students is generally similar, suggesting that gender does not significantly affect Mathematics performance.

  1. Comparison by Gender
ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = GENDER, y = MATHS, fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot()

Insight: The median scores are similar between genders.

  1. Relationship between Subjects
ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Insight: There is a positive relationship between Mathematics and English scores.
